nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Authentication helpers handling user registration, login with JWT issuance, and bearer-token verification using bcrypt password hashing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bcrypt for password hashing and PyJWT for stateless token auth, with credentials/secret read from environment variables.

nodes.name: register_user
nodes.summary: Creates a new user after checking for an existing email, hashing the password with bcrypt before persisting via the DB context.

nodes.name: login_user
nodes.summary: Validates user credentials with bcrypt and issues a signed JWT access token with an expiry claim on success.

nodes.name: verify_token
nodes.summary: Parses a Bearer authorization header and decodes the JWT to extract the authenticated user id.

nodes.name: create_tables.py
nodes.summary: Tiny bootstrap script that invokes database initialization to create all SQLAlchemy tables.

nodes.name: database.py
nodes.summary: Configures the SQLAlchemy engine and session factory, and provides a context-managed DB session plus table creation helper.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a contextmanager-style session helper that commits on success and rolls back on exception.

nodes.name: init_db
nodes.summary: Creates all database tables from the SQLAlchemy declarative Base metadata.

nodes.name: get_db_context
nodes.summary: Yields a scoped DB session that commits on success, rolls back on error, and always closes the connection.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Flask application entry point defining all HTTP routes for auth, document upload, RAG question answering, chat history, and document management.
nodes.languageNotes: Flask routes delegate auth to backend.auth and document RAG operations to backend.rag, persisting via context-managed SQLAlchemy sessions.

nodes.name: POST /auth/register
nodes.summary: Registration endpoint that validates the request body and delegates user creation to register_user.

nodes.name: POST /auth/login
nodes.summary: Login endpoint that validates credentials and delegates token issuance to login_user.

nodes.name: POST /documents/upload
nodes.summary: Authenticated endpoint that saves an uploaded PDF, chunks and embeds it into the vector store, and records the document in the database.

nodes.name: POST /documents/ask
nodes.summary: Authenticated RAG endpoint that answers a question against a user's document and persists the Q&A with sources to chat history.

nodes.name: GET /documents/history
nodes.summary: Authenticated endpoint returning the chat history records for the current user.

nodes.name: GET /documents
nodes.summary: Authenticated endpoint listing the documents uploaded by the current user.

nodes.name: DELETE /documents/<int:document_id>
nodes.summary: Authenticated endpoint that deletes a user's document, its chat history, and the underlying file from disk.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM models defining the User, Document, and ChatHistory tables and their relationships.
nodes.languageNotes: Declarative SQLAlchemy models extending a shared Base, mapping users, uploaded documents, and chat Q&A history.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: ORM model representing an application user with email and hashed password.

nodes.name: Document
nodes.summary: ORM model representing an uploaded document owned by a user, including filename and upload timestamp.

nodes.name: ChatHistory
nodes.summary: ORM model storing a user's question, the generated answer, sources, and timestamp linked to a document.

nodes.name: test_auth.py
nodes.summary: Pytest suite for the auth module covering registration, duplicate-email rejection, login token issuance, invalid password handling, and token verification using an in-memory SQLite session.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pytest fixtures with monkeypatch to swap the DB context for an in-memory SQLite session.

nodes.name: db_session
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture providing an in-memory SQLite session and monkeypatching the production DB context to use it.

nodes.name: test_register_user_creates_user
nodes.summary: Verifies register_user persists a new user row to the database.

nodes.name: test_register_user_rejects_duplicate_email
nodes.summary: Verifies register_user rejects registration when the email already exists.

nodes.name: test_login_user_returns_token
nodes.summary: Verifies login_user returns a valid JWT that verify_token can decode for a seeded user.

nodes.name: test_login_user_rejects_invalid_password
nodes.summary: Verifies login_user rejects authentication when the supplied password is incorrect.

nodes.name: test_verify_token_accepts_bearer_token
nodes.summary: Verifies verify_token correctly decodes a valid Bearer-prefixed JWT.

nodes.name: test_main.py
nodes.summary: Pytest suite for the Flask app exercising the register validation, login route delegation, and chat history routes using the test client and monkeypatched dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Flask's test_client fixture and monkeypatch to stub DB context and auth helpers for route-level tests.

nodes.name: test_login_route_delegates_to_login_user
nodes.summary: Verifies the login route delegates to login_user by monkeypatching it and asserting the response.

nodes.name: test_history_route_returns_user_chat_history
nodes.summary: Verifies the history route returns a user's chat history using stubbed DB context and token verification.

nodes.name: App.jsx
nodes.summary: Router root for the React SPA defining routes for login, dashboard, and chat pages, with a PrivateRoute guard that checks for a JWT token in localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses react-router-dom route configuration with a higher-order PrivateRoute wrapper for client-side auth gating.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Top-level component declaring the application's route table, wrapping protected routes in PrivateRoute.

nodes.name: client.js
nodes.summary: Axios API client that centralizes all backend calls (auth, document upload/list/delete, chat) and attaches the JWT bearer token to outgoing requests.
nodes.languageNotes: Axios instance with a request interceptor injecting the Authorization header from localStorage.

nodes.name: uploadDocument
nodes.summary: Uploads a PDF file to the backend by building a multipart FormData payload and POSTing it to the documents endpoint.

nodes.name: Navbar.jsx
nodes.summary: Navigation bar component showing the logged-in user and providing a logout action that clears the JWT token and redirects to login.

nodes.name: Navbar
nodes.summary: Renders the top navigation with a logout handler that removes auth credentials from localStorage and navigates to the login page.

nodes.name: main.jsx
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap that mounts the root App component into the DOM and loads global stylesheet.
nodes.languageNotes: Vite/React 18 entry using ReactDOM.createRoot for concurrent rendering.

nodes.name: Chat.jsx
nodes.summary: Chat page that lets users ask questions about a selected document, rendering message history with source citations and auto-scrolling to the latest reply.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useState/useEffect/useRef hooks; auto-scroll via scrollIntoView on a ref sentinel element.

nodes.name: Chat
nodes.summary: Chat page component managing question input, loading/error state, message list, and submitting questions to the backend via askQuestion.

nodes.name: Dashboard.jsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard page for managing PDF documents: fetches the document list, handles uploads with progress/feedback, supports deletion, and navigates to chat per document.
nodes.languageNotes: Coordinates multiple useState slices for upload, delete, and loading status with optimistic list updates.

nodes.name: Dashboard
nodes.summary: Dashboard component that fetches, uploads, and deletes documents through the API client and renders the document list with per-item actions.

nodes.name: Login.jsx
nodes.summary: Login/registration page that authenticates users via the API client, stores the returned JWT and user info in localStorage, and redirects to the dashboard.

nodes.name: Login
nodes.summary: Login component handling both registration and login flows, persisting auth tokens to localStorage and navigating to the dashboard on success.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Container definition for the Python Flask backend, building on python:3.12-slim, installing requirements, exposing port 5000, and launching main.py.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build using the slim Python base image with --no-cache-dir to keep the image small.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion list that keeps git metadata, Python caches, virtualenvs, secrets, uploads, logs, and the Chroma vector store out of the backend image.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template for environment variables defining the database connection, OpenAI API key, JWT secret, and Flask runtime settings required by the backend.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Minimal backend package manifest declaring Node-level dependencies for the Flask-based backend tooling.

nodes.name: rag.py
nodes.summary: Core RAG pipeline built on LangChain, OpenAI embeddings, and ChromaDB: loads and chunks PDFs, embeds and persists vectors, and answers questions by retrieving relevant context and invoking ChatOpenAI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LangChain Expression Language (LCEL) to compose a retrieval chain via the pipe operator with RunnablePassthrough and StrOutputParser.

nodes.name: load_and_chunk
nodes.summary: Loads a PDF from disk with PyPDFLoader and splits it into overlapping text chunks using RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter.

nodes.name: embed_and_store
nodes.summary: Generates OpenAI embeddings for document chunks and persists them in a per-document ChromaDB collection.

nodes.name: get_vector_store
nodes.summary: Returns a Chroma vector store bound to the collection for a given document id, configured with OpenAI embeddings.

nodes.name: answer_question
nodes.summary: Answers a user question by retrieving relevant chunks from the document's vector store and running an LCEL chain with ChatOpenAI, returning the answer along with source documents.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency manifest pinning the backend stack including Flask, LangChain, OpenAI, and ChromaDB packages.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration for the React frontend, enabling recommended rules plus React Hooks and React Refresh linting.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry document for the Vite-powered React app, mounting the application root and loading the main script module.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend package manifest declaring the React/Vite app's scripts, runtime dependencies, and dev tooling.

nodes.name: App.css
nodes.summary: Application-level stylesheet defining layout and component styling for the main App views.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global base stylesheet defining root variables, typography, and resets imported at the app entry point.

nodes.name: vite.config.js
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration enabling the React plugin for the frontend development and production builds.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview documenting the Enterprise Knowledge Assistant's features, tech stack, project structure, environment variables, and local setup instructions for the Flask/React RAG application.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration fixture file that adjusts the import path so backend modules can be imported during test collection.

nodes.name: test_rag.py
nodes.summary: Unit test suite for the RAG pipeline, mocking LangChain, OpenAI, and ChromaDB to verify chunking, embedding storage, vector store collection naming, and question answering with source retrieval.

nodes.name: test_load_and_chunk_returns_chunks
nodes.summary: Verifies load_and_chunk invokes PyPDFLoader and RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter correctly and returns the split chunks.

nodes.name: test_embed_and_store_persists_chunks
nodes.summary: Verifies embed_and_store constructs OpenAI embeddings and a Chroma store and persists the chunks via add_documents.

nodes.name: test_get_vector_store_uses_correct_collection_name
nodes.summary: Verifies get_vector_store builds a Chroma store using the document-specific collection name and OpenAI embeddings.

nodes.name: test_answer_question_returns_answer_and_sources
nodes.summary: Verifies answer_question returns both an answer and source documents by mocking the retriever and LCEL chain components.

nodes.name: test_answer_question_uses_retriever_search_kwargs
nodes.summary: Verifies answer_question configures the retriever with the expected search kwargs when querying the vector store.

nodes.name: SimpleChain
nodes.summary: Test double that emulates a LangChain runnable by implementing the pipe operators and invoke, allowing the LCEL chain to be exercised without real LangChain components.
